TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}

S.FORM: ka sitsuayan izua kalaingan ni Qaquangan a vavayan.
S.PHON: ka siʦuajan izua kaɭaiŋan ni qaquaŋan a vavajan.
S.FORM: ka sicuayan izua kalaingan ni Qaquangan a vavayan.
S.PHON: ka siʦuajan izua kaɣaiŋan ni qaquaŋan a vavajan.
S.TRANSL: Once upon a time there was a girl who was the oldest child in the Qaquangan family.

S.FORM: nu puvaɫaw, sa nu kiuḍe azua vaɫaw, katsen nua kutji a kulits; qau matsay a uqaɫay.
S.PHON: nu puvaʎaw, sa nu kiuɖə azua vaʎaw, kaʦən nua kuci a kuɭiʦ; qau maʦaj a uqaʎaj.
S.FORM: nu puvaljaw, sa nu kiudre azua valjaw, kacen nua kutji a kulic; qau macay a uqaljay.
S.PHON: nu puvaʎaw, sa nu kiuɖə azua vaʎaw, kaʦən nua kuʦi a kuɣiʦ; qau maʦaj a uqaʎaj.
S.TRANSL: When she married and her husband had intercourse with her, his penis was bitten by her vagina and the husband died.

S.FORM: semu maɫelima a puvaɫaw; matsay a mapuɫat.
S.PHON: səmu maʎəɭima a puvaʎaw; maʦaj a mapuʎat.
S.FORM: semu maljelima a puvaljaw; macay a mapuljat.
S.PHON: səmu maʎəɣima a puvaʎaw; maʦaj a mapuʎat.
S.TRANSL: She got through five husbands, and they all died.

S.FORM: "aku maya saka maitazua a matsay a su vaɫaw?
S.PHON: "aku maja saka maitazua a maʦaj a su vaʎaw
S.FORM: "aku maya saka maitazua a macay a su valjaw?
S.PHON: "aku maja saka maitazua a maʦaj a su vaʎaw
S.TRANSL: “Why ever is it that your husbands die like that?

S.FORM: manu namakuda sun?
S.PHON: manu namakuda sun
S.FORM: manu namakuda sun?
S.PHON: manu namakuda sun

S.FORM: tja qayamaw a nisun a kutji," ayain nua kina.
S.PHON: ca qajamaw a nisun a kuci," ajain nua kina.
S.FORM: tja qayamaw a nisun a kutji," ayain nua kina.
S.PHON: ʦa qajamaw a nisun a kuʦi," ajain nua kina.
S.TRANSL: We’ll have a look at your private parts,” her mother told her.

S.FORM: manu izuangata a aɫis.
S.PHON: manu izuaŋata a aʎis.
S.FORM: manu izuangata a aljis.
S.PHON: manu izuaŋata a aʎis.

S.FORM: qau tsingtsingan sa sualapi a aɫis.
S.PHON: qau ʦiŋʦiŋan sa suaɭapi a aʎis.
S.FORM: qau cingcingan sa sualapi a aljis.
S.PHON: qau ʦiŋʦiŋan sa suaɣapi a aʎis.
S.TRANSL: So the teeth were cut and removed.

S.FORM: azua sinualap a aɫis a ita, sa sipatsusuan anga tua zangaq.
S.PHON: azua sinuaɭap a aʎis a ita, sa sipaʦusuan aŋa tua zaŋaq.
S.FORM: azua sinualap a aljis a ita, sa sipacusuan anga tua zangaq.
S.PHON: azua sinuaɣap a aʎis a ita, sa sipaʦusuan aŋa tua zaŋaq.
S.TRANSL: There was one tooth removed, and it was threaded onto a necklace;

S.FORM: saka izuanan tutsu, aya a tjautsikel.
S.PHON: saka izuanan tuʦu, aja a cauʦikəɭ.
S.FORM: saka izuanan tucu, aya a tjaucikel.
S.PHON: saka izuanan tuʦu, aja a ʦauʦikəɣ.
S.TRANSL: and it still exists, according to the story.
